C003.claim: This framework lets us calculate how quantum systems evolve and interact.
C003.query: Hilbert space This framework lets us calculate how quantum systems evolve and interact. physics lecture notes textbook explanation
C003.result_snippet: In this chapter we describe the Hilbert space formulation of Quantum Physics. The first 10 sections contain general formalisms which apply to all Quantum Physics, the other 6 sections are specific for mechanical quantum systems. In physics, Hilbert spaces often occur as spaces of functions on some space. We have already seen the examples of L 2 (R) and L 2 ([0, 1]), the Hilbert spaces of square-integrable functions on the real line and the unit interval, respectively. Now that we’ve established what features are needed in quantum mechanics - namely, interference effects and complex-valued physical quantities - we’ll learn a bit about the mathematics of Hilbert spaces, which will give us the language we need to understand these effects. We’ll now understand how quantum mechanics handles systems with more than one degree of freedom by taking the tensor product of the Hilbert spaces of its constituent parts. Abstract: Hilbert spaces provide the fundamental mathematical framework for describing quantum mechanical systems.
